description: Andrew Key was appointed Director of Government Skills in February 2024. Before this he was Chief Executive of the Judicial Office, supporting the Lady Chief Justice and Senior President of Tribunals in their leadership of the judiciary and relations with government, parliament and the public.  He has spent much of his career in the Foreign Office, including as Deputy Head of Mission in Beijing, Assistant Director for Human Resources and Ambassador to Macedonia.  The Director of Government Skills leads work to ensure the civil service has the skills needed to deliver the government’s priorities, now and for the future. Areas of responsibility include provision of learning across the civil service, leadership development and accelerated development schemes through the Leadership College for Government, oversight of the College for National Security, and development and delivery of the civil service Fast Stream.

details.body:
Andrew Key was appointed Director of Government Skills in February 2024.

Before this he was Chief Executive of the Judicial Office, supporting the Lady Chief Justice and Senior President of Tribunals in their leadership of the judiciary and relations with government, parliament and the public. 

He has spent much of his career in the Foreign Office, including as Deputy Head of Mission in Beijing, Assistant Director for Human Resources and Ambassador to Macedonia. 

The Director of Government Skills leads work to ensure the civil service has the skills needed to deliver the government’s priorities, now and for the future. Areas of responsibility include provision of learning across the civil service, leadership development and accelerated development schemes through the Leadership College for Government, oversight of the College for National Security, and development and delivery of the civil service Fast Stream.

He has spent much of his career in the Foreign Office, including as Deputy Head of Mission in Beijing, Assistant Director for Human Resources and Ambassador to Macedonia. 

The Director of Government Skills leads work to ensure the civil service has the skills needed to deliver the government’s priorities, now and for the future. Areas of responsibility include provision of learning across the civil service, leadership development and accelerated development schemes through the Leadership College for Government, oversight of the College for National Security, and development and delivery of the civil service Fast Stream.

role_appointments.title: Andrew Key - Director of Government Skills

details.body:
Andrew Key was appointed Director of Government Skills in February 2024.

Before this he was Chief Executive of the Judicial Office, supporting the Lady Chief Justice and Senior President of Tribunals in their leadership of the judiciary and relations with government, parliament and the public. 

He has spent much of his career in the Foreign Office, including as Deputy Head of Mission in Beijing, Assistant Director for Human Resources and Ambassador to Macedonia. 

The Director of Government Skills leads work to ensure the civil service has the skills needed to deliver the government’s priorities, now and for the future. Areas of responsibility include provision of learning across the civil service, leadership development and accelerated development schemes through the Leadership College for Government, oversight of the College for National Security, and development and delivery of the civil service Fast Stream.

The Deputy Head of Mission is a senior diplomat and typically a key advisor to the Ambassador or High Commissioner. The Deputy is responsible for the daily management of an overseas Embassy or High Commission. They will represent the UK’s interests in the absence of the Ambassador as Chargé d’Affaires. A senior ranking Deputy may also take the title Minister. Smaller missions may not have a Deputy Head.

role.title: Deputy Head of Mission, British Embassy Beijing
